rule_set.text_or_description: Federal Court has exclusive jurisdiction over constitutional references (Article 128) and is the apex appellate court for civil and criminal matters.
rule_set.legal_source: Federal Constitution Articles 121(2), 128; Courts of Judicature Act ss. 81-99

rule_set.text_or_description: Judicial power was textually removed from Article 121(1) by Constitution (Amendment) Act 1988; subsequent rulings (Semenyih Jaya 2017, Indira Gandhi 2018) reasserted basic-structure doctrine.
rule_set.legal_source: Federal Constitution Article 121(1) as amended 1988; case law
rule_set.enforcement_mechanism: Doctrinal — judicial review of own jurisdiction

rule_set.text_or_description: State List boundary: state Shariah enactments must not encroach on federal subjects (Ninth Schedule List I and List II). Nik Elin (Federal Court, 2024) voided Kelantan provisions on this ground.

notes: Established 1 January 1985 (Privy Council appeals abolished by the Constitution Amendment Act 1985). 1988 crisis fundamentally altered constitutional doctrine on judicial power. Recent jurisprudence (post-2017) has restored some independence; Nik Elin (2024) is the cleanest recent assertion of Federal Court authority on State List boundaries.
